from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of triples about an athlete into a single fluent sentence."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Simple mapping from predicate to a natural‑language fragment.
    phrase_map = {
        "birthDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born on {o}",
        "birthPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {o}",
        "birthYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {o}",
        "height": lambda s, o: f"{s} is {o} metres tall",
        "weight": lambda s, o: f"{s} weighs {o}",
        "club": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays for {o}",
        "currentclub": lambda s, o: f"{s} currently plays for {o}",
        "formerTeam": lambda s, o: f"{s} formerly played for {o}",
        "position": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays as a {o}",
        "league": lambda s, o: f"{s} competes in the {o} league",
        "manager": lambda s, o: f"{s} is managed by {o}",
        "coach": lambda s, o: f"{s} is coached by {o}",
        "ground": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s home ground is {o}",
        "stadium": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s stadium is {o}",
        "draftYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} was drafted in {o}",
        "draftRound": lambda s, o: f"{s} was selected in round {o}",
        "draftPick": lambda s, o: f"{s} was the {o}th pick",
        "college": lambda s, o: f"{s} attended {o}",
        "youthclub": lambda s, o: f"{s} started at youth club {o}",
        "playerNumber": lambda s, o: f"{s} wears number {o}",
        "activeYearsStartYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} started his professional career in {o}",
        "debutTeam": lambda s, o: f"{s} debuted with {o}",
        "currentteam": lambda s, o: f"{s} currently plays for {o}",
        "draftTeam": lambda s, o: f"{s} was drafted by {o}",
        # Fallback for any other predicate
        "default": lambda s, p, o: f"{s} {p} {o}",
    }

    # Group triples by their subject to build concise clauses.
    subj_map: dict[str, list[tuple[str, str]]] = {}
    for triple in triples:
        subj_map.setdefault(triple.subject, []).append((triple.predicate, triple.object))

    clause_sentences: list[str] = []
    for subj, facts in subj_map.items():
        clause_parts: list[str] = []
        for pred, obj in facts:
            formatter = phrase_map.get(pred, None)
            if formatter:
                try:
                    txt = formatter(subj, obj)
                except TypeError:
                    txt = phrase_map["default"](subj, pred, obj)
            else:
                txt = phrase_map["default"](subj, pred, obj)

            # Remove the leading subject if the formatter already inserted it,
            # so we can concatenate multiple facts about the same subject cleanly.
            if txt.startswith(f"{subj} "):
                txt = txt[len(subj) + 1 :]
            clause_parts.append(txt)

        # Build a sentence for this subject.
        if clause_parts:
            first_part = f"{subj} {clause_parts[0]}"
            if len(clause_parts) > 1:
                # Join remaining parts with commas and a final conjunction.
                rest = ", ".join(clause_parts[1:-1])
                last = clause_parts[-1]
                if rest:
                    sentence_body = f"{first_part}, {rest}, and {last}"
                else:
                    sentence_body = f"{first_part} and {last}"
            else:
                sentence_body = first_part
            clause_sentences.append(sentence_body)

    # Join sentences for different subjects with a semicolon for readability.
    final_sentence = "; ".join(clause_sentences) + "."
    return final_sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of triples about an athlete into a single fluent sentence."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Simple mapping from predicate to a natural‑language fragment.
    phrase_map = {
        "birthDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born on {o}",
        "birthPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {o}",
        "birthYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {o}",
        "height": lambda s, o: f"{s} is {o} metres tall",
        "weight": lambda s, o: f"{s} weighs {o}",
        "club": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays for {o}",
        "currentclub": lambda s, o: f"{s} currently plays for {o}",
        "formerTeam": lambda s, o: f"{s} formerly played for {o}",
        "position": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays as a {o}",
        "league": lambda s, o: f"{s} competes in the {o} league",
        "manager": lambda s, o: f"{s} is managed by {o}",
        "coach": lambda s, o: f"{s} is coached by {o}",
        "ground": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s home ground is {o}",
        "stadium": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s stadium is {o}",
        "draftYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} was drafted in {o}",
        "draftRound": lambda s, o: f"{s} was selected in round {o}",
        "draftPick": lambda s, o: f"{s} was the {o}th pick",
        "college": lambda s, o: f"{s} attended {o}",
        "youthclub": lambda s, o: f"{s} started at youth club {o}",
        "playerNumber": lambda s, o: f"{s} wears number {o}",
        "activeYearsStartYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} started his professional career in {o}",
        "debutTeam": lambda s, o: f"{s} debuted with {o}",
        "currentteam": lambda s, o: f"{s} currently plays for {o}",
        "draftTeam": lambda s, o: f"{s} was drafted by {o}",
        # Fallback for any other predicate
        "default": lambda s, p, o: f"{s} {p} {o}",
    }

    parts = []
    for triple in triples:
        subj, pred, obj = triple.subject, triple.predicate, triple.object
        # Use the specific formatter if available, otherwise fallback.
        formatter = phrase_map.get(pred, None)
        if formatter:
            # Some formatters expect only subject and object.
            try:
                part = formatter(subj, obj)
            except TypeError:
                # For the generic default that needs predicate as well.
                part = phrase_map["default"](subj, pred, obj)
        else:
            part = phrase_map["default"](subj, pred, obj)
        parts.append(part)

    # Combine parts into a fluent sentence, merging repeated subjects.
    if not parts:
        return ""
    sentence = parts[0]
    for part in parts[1:]:
        # Identify subjects (text before first space)
        cur_subj = part.split(' ', 1)[0]
        prev_subj = sentence.split(' ', 1)[0]
        if cur_subj == prev_subj:
            # Omit the repeated subject and connect with a conjunction
            rest = part[len(cur_subj):].lstrip()
            sentence += f", and {rest}"
        else:
            sentence += f". {part}"
    # Ensure single trailing period
    sentence = sentence.rstrip('.')
    sentence += "."
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
